observations.polarity: Unconscious action on left, conscious action on right
observations.summary: The passage contrasts the unconscious, automatic nature of the hand finding the Zuna stone with the implied conscious actions that might be expected in such a moment, highlighting a tension between instinctive familiarity and deliberate intent.

left_evidence.quote: His hand simply found it the way a hand finds familiar things in darkness.

right_evidence.quote: He didn't reach for it consciously.
